verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46) is a single-pass TM glycoprotein resident in the trans-Golgi network that continuously cycles through the plasma membrane en route back to the TGN via endosomes. It reaches the outer leaflet of the PM during each trafficking cycle, making it technically accessible from the extracellular face as a transient pool. Surface biotinylation and some surface proteomics studies detect it on intact cells. However, the dominant steady-state compartment is the TGN. This cycling PM pool qualifies as dual_localization: the protein has its own TM anchor and does transiently appear on the outer leaflet, even though rapid clathrin-mediated internalization keeps steady-state surface levels very low.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The PM-cycling pool of TGN46 is very transient and steady-state surface levels are extremely low; uncertain whether this constitutes meaningful surface accessibility for therapeutic binders.
